generatedSentence: Dr. Aris, a newbie academic and our favorite researcher, often worked at an adagio pace, dreaming of unraveling ancient mysteries. He kept doodles of an armadillo and a kinkajou—his pet nicknames for nature's curious creatures—on his desk. Despite his intellect, a deep humility guided his study of earthen artifacts. He'd sometimes snack on jelly or nougat, contemplating the slow harvest of knowledge. He found renewed wonder in every discovery, much like a kindergartner learning to read. But the quiet was shattered. A looming deadline for his funding left Aris failing to make sufficient progress, causing his temper to fray. Reports of a mysterious 'killer' phenomenon, leaving behind gnarly twisted metal and strange fallout, began to make headline news. He observed a jerboa darting away from a rusted section of railroads, a strange sight. The local authorities began the removal of any evidence they deemed dangerous, forcing Aris to disobey their warnings and investigate secretly. He felt the need to work faster, a desperate race against time. He knew he had to evaluate the evidence himself. Inside the facility, goosebumps prickled Aris's neck as he faced the source of the anomaly: a massive, helix-shaped device humming with dark energy. Robotic 'guard jaguars' were jumping and jabbing at the perimeter, preventing escape. Aris felt an unsettling power, almost like telekinesis, radiating from the core, making the air thicken. He had to estimate its destructive potential. He saw a small container labeled "FUDGE - emergency rations," ironically placed next to an energy conduit resembling a strange eggplant. Suddenly, a rocket-powered drone, resembling a grotesque koala with a rapier arm, shot towards him. "Avast!" he yelled, ready for the fight. With the device neutralized, Aris took a moment to reflect. The ordeal had been a profound experience, revealing his greatest strength wasn't just his intellect, but his resolve, overcoming his perceived handicap for field work. He returned to his lab, where a small gecko greeted him from a potted kiwi plant. He realized he needed to paraphrase his findings, simplifying the complex technical jargon for broader understanding. He eagerly sought new ways to communicate, perhaps even using packaged metaphors to make his research more accessible. He envisioned a future where even the most esoteric animal behaviors, like a petrel panning for food, could be explained clearly. He'd even tried braiding twine to represent complex data structures, and was now tabbing through research on the nutritional value of garlic and pistachios, ready to gaffing new knowledge from the depths of the unknown, always seeking to share his naansense with the world.
panelSentences: Dr. Aris, a newbie academic and our favorite researcher, often worked at an adagio pace, dreaming of unraveling ancient mysteries. He kept doodles of an armadillo and a kinkajou—his pet nicknames for nature's curious creatures—on his desk. Despite his intellect, a deep humility guided his study of earthen artifacts. He'd sometimes snack on jelly or nougat, contemplating the slow harvest of knowledge. He found renewed wonder in every discovery, much like a kindergartner learning to read.

But the quiet was shattered. A looming deadline for his funding left Aris failing to make sufficient progress, causing his temper to fray. Reports of a mysterious 'killer' phenomenon, leaving behind gnarly twisted metal and strange fallout, began to make headline news. He observed a jerboa darting away from a rusted section of railroads, a strange sight. The local authorities began the removal of any evidence they deemed dangerous, forcing Aris to disobey their warnings and investigate secretly. He felt the need to work faster, a desperate race against time. He knew he had to evaluate the evidence himself.

Inside the facility, goosebumps prickled Aris's neck as he faced the source of the anomaly: a massive, helix-shaped device humming with dark energy. Robotic 'guard jaguars' were jumping and jabbing at the perimeter, preventing escape. Aris felt an unsettling power, almost like telekinesis, radiating from the core, making the air thicken. With the device neutralized, Aris took a moment to reflect. The ordeal had been a profound experience, revealing his greatest strength wasn't just his intellect, but his resolve, overcoming his perceived handicap for field work. He returned to his lab, where a small gecko greeted him from a potted kiwi plant. He realized he needed to paraphrase his findings, simplifying the complex technical jargon for broader understanding. He eagerly sought new ways to communicate, perhaps even using packaged metaphors to make his research more accessible. He envisioned a future where even the most esoteric animal behaviors, like a petrel panning for food, could be explained clearly. He'd even tried braiding twine to represent complex data structures, and was now tabbing through research on the nutritional value of garlic and pistachios, ready to gaffing new knowledge from the depths of the unknown, always seeking to share his naansense with the world.
rawPanelSentences: Dr. Aris, a **newbie academic** and our **favorite** researcher, often worked at an **adagio** pace, **dreaming** of unraveling ancient mysteries. He kept **doodles** of an **armadillo** and a **kinkajou**—his pet **nicknames** for nature's curious creatures—on his desk. Despite his intellect, a deep **humility** guided his study of **earthen** artifacts. He'd sometimes snack on **jelly** or **nougat**, contemplating the slow **harvest** of knowledge. He found renewed wonder in every discovery, much like a **kindergartner** learning to read.

But the quiet was shattered. A looming **deadline** for his funding left Aris **failing** to make sufficient progress, causing his **temper** to fray. Reports of a mysterious '**killer**' phenomenon, leaving behind **gnarly** twisted metal and strange **fallout**, began to make **headline** news. He observed a **jerboa** **darting** away from a **rusted** section of **railroads**, a strange sight. The local authorities began the **removal** of any evidence they deemed dangerous, forcing Aris to **disobey** their warnings and investigate secretly. He felt the need to work **faster**, a desperate race against time. He knew he had to **evaluate** the evidence himself.

Inside the facility, **goosebumps** prickled Aris's **neck** as he faced the source of the anomaly: a massive, **helix**-shaped device humming with dark energy. Robotic 'guard **jaguars**' were **jumping** and **jabbing** at the perimeter, preventing escape. Aris felt an unsettling power, almost like **telekinesis**, radiating from the core, making the air **thicken**. He had to **estimate** its destructive potential. He saw a small container labeled "**FUDGE** - emergency rations," ironically placed next to an energy conduit resembling a strange **eggplant**. Suddenly, a **rocket**-powered drone, resembling a grotesque **koala** with a **rapier** arm, shot towards him. "**Avast**!" he yelled, ready for the fight.

With the device neutralized, Aris took a moment to reflect. The ordeal had been a profound experience, revealing his greatest strength wasn't just his intellect, but his resolve, overcoming his perceived **handicap** for field work. He **eagerly** sought new ways to communicate, perhaps even using **packaged** metaphors to make his research more accessible. He envisioned a future where even the most esoteric **animal** behaviors, like a **petrel** **panning** for food, could be explained clearly. He'd even tried braiding **twine** to represent complex data structures, and was now **tabbing** through research on the nutritional value of **garlic** and **pistachios**, ready to **gaffing** new knowledge from the depths of the unknown, always seeking to share his **naans**ense with the world.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dynamic action comic art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist: Dr. Aris, wearing a tweed jacket with patched elbows, a high-tech wrist-mounted scanner, and round spectacles. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Dr. Aris pondering an ancient earthen relic on his desk, a small gecko on a nearby plant. Medium shot, slight low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Dr. Aris crouching in the foreground, observing the smoking, twisted metal of rusted railroads in the distance, a small jerboa darting by. Wide shot, dramatic angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Dr. Aris deflecting a rapier arm from a rocket-powered koala drone, the helix device pulsing ominously in the background. Dynamic close-up, dutch angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Dr. Aris smiling confidently, holding a kiwi fruit from a small plant, a small gecko perched on his shoulder, warm lighting. Eye-level close-up. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: A type of very sweet candy or confection, usually made from sugar, butter, and milk or cream. Often used in the US synonymously with chocolate fudge.
definitions.example: Have you tried the vanilla fudge? It's delicious!

definitions.definition: Light or frothy nonsense.
A deliberately misleading or vague answer.
A made-up story.
A less than perfect decision or solution; an attempt to fix an incorrect solution after the fact.
To try to avoid giving a direct answer.
definitions.example: When I asked them if they had been at the party, they fudged.

definitions.definition: To alter something from its true state, as to hide a flaw or uncertainty. Always deliberate, but not necessarily dishonest or immoral.
definitions.example: Do you fudge your age?

definitions.definition: To botch or bungle something.
To cheat, especially in the game of marbles.
(minced oath) Used in place of fuck.
Nonsense; tommyrot.
Firmly or securely fixed in place; stable.
definitions.example: That rope is dangerously loose. Make it fast!

definitions.definition: Firm against attack; fortified by nature or art; impregnable; strong.
(of people) Steadfast, with unwavering feeling. (Now mostly in set phrases like fast friend(s).)
Moving with great speed, or capable of doing so; swift, rapid.
definitions.example: I am going to buy a fast car.

definitions.definition: Causing unusual rapidity of play or action.
definitions.example: a fast billiard table

definitions.definition: (of a piece of hardware) Able to transfer data in a short period of time.
Deep or sound (of sleep); fast asleep (of people).
(of dyes or colours) Not running or fading when subjected to detrimental conditions such as wetness or intense light; permanent.
definitions.example: All the washing has come out pink. That red tee-shirt was not fast.

definitions.definition: Tenacious; retentive.
Having an extravagant lifestyle or immoral habits.
definitions.example: a fast woman

definitions.definition: Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
definitions.example: There must be something wrong with the hall clock. It is always fast.

definitions.definition: (of photographic film) More sensitive to light than average.
In a firm or secure manner, securely; in such a way as not to be moved; safe, sound .
definitions.example: Hold this rope as fast as you can.

definitions.definition: (of sleeping) Deeply or soundly .
definitions.example: He is fast asleep.

definitions.definition: Immediately following in place or time; close, very near .
definitions.example: Fast by the sturdy batsman the ball unheeded sped. / That ain't my style, said Casey. Strike one, the umpire said.

definitions.definition: Quickly, with great speed; within a short time .
definitions.example: Do it as fast as you can.

definitions.definition: Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
definitions.example: I think my watch is running fast.

definitions.definition: To be unsuccessful.
definitions.example: Throughout my life, I have always failed.

definitions.definition: Not to achieve a particular stated goal. (Usage note: The direct object of this word is usually an infinitive.)
definitions.example: The truck failed to start.

definitions.definition: To neglect.
definitions.example: The report fails to take into account all the mitigating factors.

definitions.definition: Of a machine, etc.: to cease to operate correctly.
definitions.example: After running five minutes, the engine failed.

definitions.definition: To be wanting to, to be insufficient for, to disappoint, to desert.
To receive one or more non-passing grades in academic pursuits.
definitions.example: I failed English last year.

definitions.definition: To give a student a non-passing grade in an academic endeavour.
definitions.example: The professor failed me because I did not complete any of the course assignments.

definitions.definition: To miss attaining; to lose.
To be wanting; to fall short; to be or become deficient in any measure or degree up to total absence.
definitions.example: The crops failed last year.

definitions.definition: To be affected with want; to come short; to lack; to be deficient or unprovided; used with of.
To fall away; to become diminished; to decline; to decay; to sink.
To deteriorate in respect to vigour, activity, resources, etc.; to become weaker.
definitions.example: A sick man fails.

definitions.definition: To perish; to die; used of a person.
To err in judgment; to be mistaken.
To become unable to meet one's engagements; especially, to be unable to pay one's debts or discharge one's business obligation; to become bankrupt or insolvent.
Weakness; defect
definitions.example: His worst failing is his temper.

definitions.definition: If the preferred or prior option is not possible
definitions.example: A large proportion of the females employed in other firms are said to have signified their intention of going on strike, failing a settlement.

definitions.definition: The event of small airborne particles falling to the ground in significant quantities as a result of major industrial activity, volcano eruption, sandstorm, nuclear explosion, etc.
The particles themselves.
definitions.example: On 26 April 1986 the reactor number four at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant located in the Soviet Union near Pripyat in Ukraine exploded. Further explosions and the resulting fire sent a plume of highly radioactive fallout into the atmosphere and over an extensive geographical area.

definitions.definition: A negative side effect; an undesirable or unexpected consequence.
definitions.example: Psychological fallout in the shadow of terrorism, title of an article by Dr. Abraham Twerski, M.D. in http//www.jewishworldreview.com.

definitions.definition: A declined offer in a sales transaction when acceptance was presumed.
The person who declines such an offer.
An impromptu guest used to fill in for another guest spot who is a no show or who has cancelled last minute
A person or thing who enjoys special regard or favour.
definitions.example: The teacher's favourite always went first.

definitions.definition: A person who is preferred or trusted above all others.
A contestant or competitor thought most likely to win.
definitions.example: She's the favourite, she'll probably be elected.

definitions.definition: A bookmark in a web browser.
(in the plural) A short curl dangling over the temples, fashionable in the reign of Charles II.
To favour.
To bookmark.
To add to one's list of favourite items on a website that allows users to compile such lists.
Preferred or liked above all others (unless qualified).
definitions.example: This is my second favourite occupation.

definitions.definition: Belonging to a category whose members are all preferred or liked over nonmembers.
definitions.example: I just saw a movie with all my favourite actors in it.

definitions.definition: To oxidize, especially of iron or steel.
definitions.example: The patio furniture had rusted in the wind-driven spray.

definitions.definition: To cause to oxidize.
definitions.example: The wind-driven spray had thoroughly rusted the patio furniture.

definitions.definition: To be affected with the parasitic fungus called rust.
To (cause to) degenerate in idleness; to make or become dull or impaired by inaction.
Corroded; having been oxidized or covered in rust.
A permanent road consisting of fixed metal rails to drive trains or similar motorized vehicles on.
definitions.example: Many railroads roughly follow the trace of older land - and/or water roads

definitions.definition: The transportation system comprising such roads and vehicles fitted to travel on the rails, usually with several vehicles connected together in a train.
A single, privately or publicly owned property comprising one or more such roads and usually associated assets
definitions.example: Railroads can only compete fully if their tracks are technically compatible with and linked to each-other

definitions.definition: A procedure conducted in haste without due consideration.
definitions.example: The lawyers made the procedure a railroad to get the signatures they needed.

definitions.definition: To transport via railroad.
To operate a railroad.
definitions.example: The Thatcherite experiment proved the private sector can railroad as inefficiently as a state monopoly

definitions.definition: To work for a railroad.
To travel by railroad.
To engage in a hobby pertaining to railroads.
To manipulate and hasten a procedure, as of formal approval of a law or resolution.
definitions.example: The majority railroaded the bill through parliament, without the customary expert studies which would delay it till after the elections.

definitions.definition: To convict of a crime by circumventing due process.
definitions.example: They could only convict him by railroading him on suspect drug-possession charges.

definitions.definition: To procedurally bully someone into an unfair agreement.
definitions.example: He was railroaded into signing a non-disclosure agreement at his exit interview.

definitions.definition: To force characters to complete a task before allowing the plot to continue.
(upholstery) To run fabric horizontally instead of the usual vertically.
The process of moving, or the fact of being removed.
The relocation of a business etc.
The dismissal of someone from office.

An evening funeral ritual in which the coffin holding the deceased is brought, usually from a funeral home, to the church where the funeral mass will be celebrated the following day. Prayers are said before and after the journey, after which mourners are typically received at the home of the deceased.
Murder.
A slender, straight, sharply pointed sword (double-edged, single-edged or edgeless).
Extremely sharp.
Cutting; employing keen wit.
definitions.example: John is very quick on his feet during interviews by using his rapier responses.

definitions.definition: A rocket engine.
A non-guided missile propelled by a rocket engine.
A vehicle propelled by a rocket engine.
A rocket propelled firework, a skyrocket
An ace (the playing card).
An angry communication (such as a letter or telegram) to a subordinate.
A blunt lance head used in jousting.
Something that shoots high in the air.
A stupid or crazy person.
To accelerate swiftly and powerfully
To fly vertically
To rise or soar rapidly
To carry something in a rocket
To attack something with rockets
A child who attends a kindergarten.
A person who teaches at a kindergarten.
Potos flavus, a carnivorous mammal of Central America and South America with a long, prehensile tail, related to the raccoon.
One who or that which kills.
definitions.example: Carbon monoxide is a silent killer.

definitions.definition: That which causes stress or is extremely difficult, especially that which may cause failure at a task.
definitions.example: That test was a killer.

definitions.definition: Something that is so far ahead of its competition that it effectively kills off that competition.
definitions.example: Digital streaming platforms are CD killers.

definitions.definition: A knockout form of darts or pool involving several players.
A club used for killing fish.
A particularly heavy type of handstamp, or portion of one, often obscuring a large part of the postage stamp.

A diacritical mark used in Indic scripts to suppress an inherent vowel (e.g., the Hindi viram, the Bengali or Oriya hasanta) or render the entire syllable silent (e.g., the Burmese virama, the Khmer toandakhiat).
Excellent, very good, cool.
definitions.example: killer feature

definitions.definition: Causing death, destruction, or obliteration.
definitions.example: killer bee

definitions.definition: Distressing, uncomfortable.
definitions.example: I had a killer headache this morning.

definitions.definition: A sea mammal related to dolphins and porpoises (Orcinus orca).
A tree-dwelling marsupial, Phascolarctos cinereus, that resembles a small bear with a broad head, large ears and sharp claws, mainly found in eastern Australia.
A New Zealander.
A flightless bird of the genus Apteryx native to New Zealand.
A New Zealand dollar.
A kiwi fruit.
A green-yellow colour, like that of kiwi flesh (also called kiwi green).
A member of the air force who does not fly.
The characteristic of being humble; humbleness in character and behavior.
The third season of the year; autumn; fall.
definitions.example: Harvest is usually very damp and rainy.

definitions.definition: The season of gathering ripened crops; specifically, the time of reaping and gathering grain.
The process of gathering the ripened crop; harvesting.
The yield of harvesting, i.e., the gathered crops or fruits.
definitions.example: This year's cotton harvest was great but the corn harvest was disastrous.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The product or result of any exertion or course of action; reward or consequences.
A modern pagan ceremony held on or around the autumn equinox, which is in the harvesting season.
To bring in a harvest; reap; glean.
To be occupied bringing in a harvest
definitions.example: Harvesting is a stressing, thirsty occupation

definitions.definition: To win, achieve a gain.
definitions.example: The rising star harvested well-deserved acclaim, even an Oscar under 21

definitions.definition: Something that prevents, hampers, or hinders.
definitions.example: Age is often a handicap.

definitions.definition: An allowance of a certain amount of time or distance in starting, granted in a race (or other contest of skill) to the competitor possessing disadvantages; or an additional weight or other hindrance imposed upon the one possessing advantages, in order to equalize, as much as possible, the chances of success.
definitions.example: A handicap in chess often involves removal of the queen's rook.

definitions.definition: (sometimes considered offensive) The disadvantage itself, in particular physical or mental disadvantages of people.
A race or similar contest in which there is an allowance of time, distance, weight, or other advantage, to equalize the chances of the competitors.
(card game) An old card game, similar to lanterloo.
To encumber with a handicap in any contest.
(by extension) To place at disadvantage.
definitions.example: The candidate was handicapped by her lack of experience.

definitions.definition: To estimate betting odds.
definitions.example: Grandpa Andy would buy the racing form the day ahead of time so he could handicap the race before he even arrived at the track.

definitions.definition: A curve on the surface of a cylinder or cone such that its angle to a plane perpendicular to the axis is constant; the three-dimensional curve seen in a screw or a spiral staircase.
A small volute under the abacus of a Corinthian capital.
The incurved rim of the external ear.
An upside down midair 360° spin in playboating.
To form or cause to form a helix.
The heading or title of a magazine or newspaper article.
definitions.example: The headline on today's newspaper reads "John Doe Wins Wood-Splitting Competition."

definitions.definition: The line at the top of a page containing the folio or number of the page.
(entertainment) The top-billed attraction.
A headrope.
(entertainment) To have top billing; to be the main attraction.
A tempo mark directing that a passage is to be played rather slowly, leisurely and gracefully.
A passage having this mark.
A male-female duet or mixed trio ballet displaying demanding balance, spins and/or lifts.
Describing a passage having this mark.
Played rather slowly.
Hold fast!; desist!; stay!.
(in imitation of pirates) listen!; pay attention!
definitions.example: Avast, ye landlubbers!

definitions.definition: Any of the burrowing mammals covered with bony, jointed, protective plates, order Cingulata, found in the Americas, especially in South America.

In scientific usage, a multicellular organism that is usually mobile, whose cells are not encased in a rigid cell wall (distinguishing it from plants and fungi) and which derives energy solely from the consumption of other organisms (distinguishing it from plants).
definitions.example: A cat is an animal, not a plant. Humans are also animals, under the scientific definition, as we are not plants.

definitions.definition: In non-scientific usage, any member of the kingdom Animalia other than a human.
In non-scientific usage, any land-living vertebrate (i.e. not fishes, insects, etc.).
A person who behaves wildly; a bestial, brutal, brutish, cruel, or inhuman person.
definitions.example: My students are animals.

definitions.definition: A person of a particular type.
definitions.example: He's a political animal.

definitions.definition: Matter, thing.
definitions.example: a whole different animal

definitions.definition: (usually capitalized) A follower of Plato, a Platonist.
A senior member of an academy, college, or university; a person who attends an academy; a person engaged in scholarly pursuits; one who is academic in practice.
A member of the Academy; an academician.
A student in a college.
Academic dress; academicals.
Academic studies.
Belonging to the school or philosophy of Plato
definitions.example: the academic sect or philosophy

definitions.definition: Belonging to an academy or other higher institution of learning; also a scholarly society or organization.
Theoretical or speculative; abstract; scholarly, literary or classical, in distinction to practical or vocational
definitions.example: I have always had an academic interest in hacking.

definitions.definition: Having little practical use or value, as by being overly detailed, unengaging, or theoretical: having no practical importance.
Having a love of or aptitude for learning.
definitions.example: I'm more academic than athletic — I get lower marks in phys. ed. than in anything else.

definitions.definition: Conforming to set rules and traditions; conventional; formalistic.
So scholarly as to be unaware of the outside world; lacking in worldliness.
Subscribing to the architectural standards of Vitruvius.
Study of humanities topics rather than science and engineering.
A newcomer, someone new to something.
A new user or participant; someone who is extremely new and inexperienced (to a game or activity). A beginner.
Anything recently introduced into a setting, especially something that replaces an older version.
A confection of honey or sugar and roasted nuts, often with other ingredients.
A familiar, invented given name for a person or thing used instead of the actual name of the person or thing.
A kind of byname that describes a person by a characteristic of that person.
A type of round, flat bread baked in a tandoor popular in South and Central Asian cuisine.
The part of the body connecting the head and the trunk found in humans and some animals.
definitions.example: Giraffes have long necks.

definitions.definition: The corresponding part in some other anatomical contexts.
The part of a shirt, dress etc., which fits a person's neck.
The tapered part of a bottle toward the opening.
The slender tubelike extension atop an archegonium, through which the sperm swim to reach the egg.
The extension of any stringed instrument on which a fingerboard is mounted
A long narrow tract of land projecting from the main body, or a narrow tract connecting two larger tracts.
A reduction in size near the end of an object, formed by a groove around it.
definitions.example: a neck forming the journal of a shaft

definitions.definition: The constriction between the root and crown of a tooth.
The gorgerin of a capital.
The small part of a gun between the chase and the swell of the muzzle.
A person's life.
definitions.example: to risk one's neck; to save someone's neck

definitions.definition: A falsehood; a lie.
Shapeshifting water spirits in Germanic mythology and folklore
To hang by the neck; strangle; kill, eliminate
definitions.example: Go neck yourself.

definitions.definition: To make love; to intently kiss or cuddle; to canoodle.
definitions.example: Alan and Betty were necking in the back of a car when Betty's dad caught them.

definitions.definition: To drink rapidly.
To decrease in diameter.
To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.
definitions.example: The rider jumped the horse over the fence.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.
To increase the height of a tower crane by inserting a section at the base of the tower and jacking up everything above it.
To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.
(smithwork) To join by a buttweld.
To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.
To start executing code from a different location, rather than following the program counter.
To flee; to make one's escape.
The act of performing a jump.
Exuberantly active; in full swing.
A carnivorous spotted large cat native to South and Central America, Panthera onca.
To poke or thrust abruptly, or to make such a motion.
To deliver a quick punch.
To give someone an injection
A motion or gesture that jabs; a jab.

Any of a number of species comprising most of the family Dipodidae, native to the deserts of Asia and northern Africa, being a small, jumping rodent with a long tufted tail, very small forefeet and very long hind legs.
An explosive mixture of nitroglycerine and nitrate absorbed onto a base of wood pulp.
A dessert made by boiling gelatine, sugar and some flavouring (often derived from fruit) and allowing it to set, known as "jello" in North America.
A clear or translucent fruit preserve, made from fruit juice and set using either naturally occurring, or added, pectin. Known as "jam" in Commonwealth English.
A savoury substance, derived from meat, that has the same texture as the dessert.
Any substance or object having the consistency of jelly.
definitions.example: calf's-foot jelly

definitions.definition: A jellyfish.
A pretty girl; a girlfriend.
A large backside, especially a woman's.
A jelly shoe.
Blood.
To wiggle like jelly.
To make jelly.
A coconut with soft or gelatinous meat inside as well as the meat itself (which is of regular quality if the coconut is immature)
(chiefly in plural) Raised skin, usually caused by the involuntary erection of hairs on the neck or arms caused by cold, excitement, or fear.
To use a gaff, especially to land a fish.
To cheat or hoax.
To gamble.
To affix or cover with gaffer tape.
The process of landing a fish with a gaff.
Any lizard of the family Gekkonidae. They are small, carnivorous, mostly nocturnal animals with large eyes and adhesive toes enabling them to climb on vertical and upside-down surfaces.
To move in the manner of a gecko; to attach to a vertical or upside-down surface.
Having or characterized by gnarls; gnarled.
Excellent; attractive.
Dangerous; difficult.
definitions.example: a gnarly problem

definitions.definition: Unpleasant, awful, ugly.
A plant, Allium sativum, related to the onion, having a pungent bulb much used in cooking.
To flavour with garlic
A deciduous tree (Pistacia vera) grown in parts of Asia for its drupaceous fruit.
The nutlike fruit of this tree.
(color) A pale green colour, like that of a pistachio seed.
A restatement of a text in different words, often to clarify meaning.
One of a certain number of Scripture passages turned into verse for use in the service of praise.
To restate something as, or to compose a paraphrase.
Any of various species of black, grey, or white seabirds in the order Procellariiformes.
To wash in a pan (of earth, sand etc. when searching for gold).
To disparage; to belittle; to put down; to criticise severely.
With "out" (to pan out), to turn out well; to be successful.
(of a contest) To beat one's opposition convincingly.
To criticize harshly a work (like a book, movie, etc.)
Of a camera, etc.: to turn horizontally.

To move the camera lens angle while continuing to expose the film, enabling a contiguous view and enrichment of context. In still-photography large-group portraits the film usually remains on a horizontal fixed plane as the lens and/or the film holder moves to expose the film laterally. The resulting image may extend a short distance laterally or as great as 360 degrees from the point where the film first began to be exposed.
(audio) To spread a sound signal into a new stereo or multichannel sound field, typically giving the impression that it is moving across the sound stage.
To join or fit together; to unite.
The act of one who pans, as for gold.
Material that has been panned.
(audio) The act by which a sound is panned.
definitions.example: The new console supports pannings and fades.

definitions.definition: Thorough criticism.
definitions.example: The director's films had received pannings from critics.

definitions.definition: To pack or bundle something.
To travel on a package holiday.
To prepare (a book, a television series, etc.), including all stages from research to production, in order to sell the result to a publisher or broadcaster.
Having been placed in a package.
To refuse or (intentionally) fail to obey an order of (somebody).
To refuse or (intentionally) fail to obey.
To see imaginary events in one's mind while sleeping.
To hope, to wish.
To daydream.
definitions.example: Stop dreaming and get back to work.

definitions.definition: To envision as an imaginary experience (usually when asleep).
definitions.example: I dreamed a vivid dream last night.

definitions.definition: To consider the possibility (of).
definitions.example: I wouldn't dream of snubbing you in public.

definitions.definition: An instance of dreaming; a dream or reverie.
To throw with a sudden effort or thrust; to hurl or launch.
To send forth suddenly or rapidly; to emit; to shoot
definitions.example: The sun darts forth his beams.

definitions.definition: To shoot with a dart, especially a tranquilizer dart
definitions.example: They had to dart the animal to get close enough to help

definitions.definition: To fly or pass swiftly, like a dart; to move rapidly in one direction; to shoot out quickly
definitions.example: The flying man darted eastward.

definitions.definition: To start and run with speed; to shoot rapidly along
definitions.example: The deer darted from the thicket.

definitions.definition: A motion or gesture that darts.
A fool, a simpleton, a mindless person.
A small mindless sketch, etc.
(sometimes childish) Penis.
To draw or scribble aimlessly.
definitions.example: The bored student doodled a submarine in his notebook.

definitions.definition: To drone like a bagpipe.
Any crossbreed of a poodle with a different breed of dog.
A time limit in the form of a date on or before which something must be completed.
definitions.example: I must make this deadline or my boss will kill me!

definitions.definition: A guideline marked on a plate for a printing press.
A line that does not move.
A boundary around a prison, prisoners crossing which would be shot.
To render an item non-mission-capable; to ground an aircraft, etc.
A tendency to be in a certain type of mood; a habitual way of thinking, behaving or reacting.
definitions.example: to have a good, bad, or calm temper

definitions.definition: State of mind; mood.
A tendency to become angry.
definitions.example: He has quite a temper when dealing with salespeople.

definitions.definition: Anger; a fit of anger.
definitions.example: an outburst of temper

definitions.definition: Calmness of mind; moderation; equanimity; composure.
definitions.example: to keep one's temper; to lose one's temper; to recover one's temper

definitions.definition: Constitution of body; the mixture or relative proportion of the four humours: blood, choler, phlegm, and melancholy.
Middle state or course; mean; medium.
The state of any compound substance which results from the mixture of various ingredients; due mixture of different qualities.
definitions.example: the temper of mortar

definitions.definition: The heat treatment to which a metal or other material has been subjected; a material that has undergone a particular heat treatment.
The state of a metal or other substance, especially as to its hardness, produced by some process of heating or cooling.
definitions.example: the temper of iron or steel

definitions.definition: (sugar manufacture) Milk of lime, or other substance, employed in the process formerly used to clarify sugar.
To moderate or control.
definitions.example: Temper your language around children.

definitions.definition: To strengthen or toughen a material, especially metal, by heat treatment; anneal.
definitions.example: Tempering is a heat treatment technique applied to metals, alloys, and glass to achieve greater toughness by increasing the strength of materials and/or ductility. Tempering is performed by a controlled reheating of the work piece to a temperature below its lower eutectic critical temperature.

definitions.definition: To sauté spices in ghee or oil to release essential oils for flavouring a dish in South Asian cuisine.
To mix clay, plaster or mortar with water to obtain the proper consistency.
To adjust, as the mathematical scale to the actual scale, or to that in actual use.
(Latinism) To govern; to manage.
To combine in due proportions; to constitute; to compose.
To mingle in due proportion; to prepare by combining; to modify, as by adding some new element; to qualify, as by an ingredient; hence, to soften; to mollify; to assuage.
To fit together; to adjust; to accommodate.
The ability to move objects with the power of one's mind.
An instance of use of such power.
To make thicker (in the sense of wider).
To make thicker (in the sense of more viscous).
definitions.example: to thicken a sauce

definitions.definition: To become thicker (in the sense of wider).
To become thicker (in the sense of more viscous).
To strengthen; to confirm.
To make more frequent.
definitions.example: to thicken blows

definitions.definition: A twist; a convolution.
A strong thread composed of two or three smaller threads or strands twisted together, and used for various purposes, as for binding small parcels, making nets, and the like; a small cord or string.
The act of twining or winding round.
Intimate and suggestive dance gyrations.
To affix with tabs; to label.
To use the Tab key on a computer to advance the cursor or move the input focus, or on a typewriter to advance the carriage.
Made of earth or mud.
Made of clay (especially said of pottery).
Earthly.
A rough calculation or assessment of the value, size, or cost of something.
(construction and business) A document (or verbal notification) specifying how much a job is likely to cost.
An upper limitation on some positive quantity.
To calculate roughly, often from imperfect data.
To judge and form an opinion of the value of, from imperfect data.
The plant Solanum melongena.
The edible fruit of the Solanum melongena: an aubergine.
A dark purple color, like that of the skin of this fruit.
A black person (used mainly by Italian-Americans).
A 180 backside rotated invert in which the front hand is planted on the lip of the halfpipe wall
In an eager manner.
To draw conclusions from examining; to assess.
definitions.example: It will take several years to evaluate the material gathered in the survey.

definitions.definition: To compute or determine the value of (an expression).
definitions.example: Evaluate this integral.

definitions.definition: To return or have a specific value.
